first-pagaraphs: The map shows where the person on probation has been, using signals (‘pings’) sent from their GPS tag.
The tag uses satellite signals to record its location. It pings around once a minute, sending this location information to the Electronic Monitoring Service.
Each ping appears as a point on the map that indicates the person’s most likely location.
guide.insert: Map data is around 95% accurate. The map may sometimes show a ping in the wrong location.
last-pagaraphs: You can filter the map to view the person’s movements for specific dates and times. The most recent activity shown is about 5 minutes behind real time.

locationAccuracy.title: Location accuracy
first-pagaraphs: Location accuracy can vary, and may be reduced if, for example, satellite signals are blocked or the tag battery is low.
Map points are surrounded by ‘confidence circles’ that show how confident the system is that the tag pinged at or near to each location.
Confidence circles cover the area the person may have been in when their tag pinged. The smaller the circle, the more confidence there is about the location.
To check a circle’s exact size, select its point to open a ‘ping card’ (pop-up box). This shows the distance from the centre to the edge of the circle in metres.
You can turn confidence circles off using the option in the top-right menu.

timeAndDate.title: Time and date of activity
first-pagaraphs: Each point has a timestamp that shows when the tag pinged in that location.
To check the timestamp, select the point and open its ping card.
To work out how long the person spent at the location, compare the point’s timestamp with that of the next point in the sequence.
You can check the order of location points by turning on ‘point numbers’ in the top right-hand menu.

directionOfTravel.title: Direction of travel
first-pagaraphs: The lines on maps show the likely path a person on probation took between locations and do not represent a confirmed route.
directionOfTravel.insert: The trail shown may not reflect the actual route taken.
last-pagaraphs: Arrows along the trail show the estimated direction of travel, based on the order of the points.
You can hide the arrows using the option in the top right-hand menu.

missingTrailData.title: Time and date of activity
first-pagaraphs: Missing trail data usually means that either the tag is not working or that it cannot get a signal.
firstBulletPoints: the person has gone somewhere with no signal, for example, in a lift or on the underground
the tag has been damaged or removed
the tag battery has run out
last-pagaraphs: Missing data will result in either:
lastBulletPoints: few or no location points on the map
a gap of more than 5 minutes between two location points in a sequence
